verdict_reasoning: SRC is a peripheral membrane protein anchored to the cytoplasmic (inner) face of the plasma membrane via N-terminal myristoylation. Although it associates with the plasma membrane and interacts with surface receptors and signaling partners, the protein body is not accessible from the extracellular space. It lacks a transmembrane domain or GPI anchor and is not stably presented on the outer leaflet under baseline conditions. The documented autophagolysosomal inversion to the outer face is a context-dependent, non-canonical mechanism specific to cancer cells, not a primary localization pathway.
